task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened and read the earthquake assessment report from the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the file 'earthquake_assessment.txt' opened in a text editor or terminal, with the building damage classifications visible (SEVERE, MODERATE, MINOR).

task_completion.description: Verify the first Severe-damage building's Name field was updated with the RED status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenMaint Building detail view or list, the first building classified as SEVERE in the report should have '| EARTHQUAKE STATUS: RED' or 'DO NOT OCCUPY' appended to its Name/Description field.

task_completion.description: Verify the second Severe-damage building's Name field was updated with the RED status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenMaint Building detail view or list, the second building classified as SEVERE in the report should have '| EARTHQUAKE STATUS: RED' or 'DO NOT OCCUPY' appended to its Name/Description field.

task_completion.description: Verify the Moderate-damage building's Name field was updated with the YELLOW status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenMaint Building detail view or list, the building classified as MODERATE in the report should have '| EARTHQUAKE STATUS: YELLOW' or 'LIMITED OCCUPANCY' appended to its Name/Description field.

task_completion.description: Verify Minor-damage buildings were updated with GREEN status (no work orders created for them).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenMaint Building detail view or list, buildings classified as MINOR should have '| EARTHQUAKE STATUS: GREEN' or 'CLEARED' appended. Crucially, NO new work orders should appear for these buildings in the Corrective Maintenance list.

task_completion.description: Verify a Critical priority work order was created for the first Severe-damage building.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenMaint Corrective Maintenance list or detail view, there should be a work order with 'EARTHQUAKE' and 'Structural Assessment' in its subject/description, assigned to the first SEVERE building, with Critical priority.

task_completion.description: Verify a Critical priority work order was created for the second Severe-damage building.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenMaint Corrective Maintenance list or detail view, there should be a second work order with 'EARTHQUAKE' and 'Structural Assessment' in its subject/description, assigned to the second SEVERE building, with Critical priority.

task_completion.description: Verify a High priority work order was created for the Moderate-damage building.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenMaint Corrective Maintenance list, there should be a work order with 'EARTHQUAKE' and 'MEP' or 'Systems Inspection' in its subject/description, assigned to the MODERATE building, with High priority.

task_completion.description: Verify a new floor for the Emergency Operations Center was created under the designated Minor-damage building.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenMaint Floor list or detail view, there should be a new floor record with code 'FLR-EOC-01' or description containing 'EOC' and 'Temporary Operations', linked to the building designated as the EOC in the assessment report.

task_completion.description: Verify three EOC rooms were created under the new EOC floor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenMaint Room list or detail view, there should be three new room records: RM-EOC-CMD (Command Room), RM-EOC-COM (Communications Hub), and RM-EOC-SUP (Supply Staging), all linked to the EOC floor.

task_completion.description: Verify the pre-existing WO-SEISMIC-TEST work order was not modified or deleted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenMaint Corrective Maintenance list, the work order 'WO-SEISMIC-TEST' for 'Annual seismic sensor calibration' should still be present with its original description, not closed, deleted, or modified. The agent should not have confused this routine calibration with the earthquake response.

integrity.description: The agent performed all actions through the OpenMaint web GUI in Firefox.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenMaint web application in a browser with the agent navigating menus, forms, and dialogs — not terminal commands, curl, or direct database manipulation.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal scripts or direct API calls to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows should not show curl commands, SQL statements, or Python scripts being executed to directly insert or modify records.

integrity.description: The agent entered data that matches the assessment report, not fabricated values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Work order descriptions, building status annotations, and EOC room names should correspond to the information in earthquake_assessment.txt, not be hallucinated or invented by the agent.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The earthquake assessment report classifies 5 buildings. The severity assignments are positional from the building list returned by the system: the 1st and 4th buildings are SEVERE, the 2nd building is MODERATE, and the 3rd and 5th buildings are MINOR. The 3rd building (first MINOR) is designated as the Emergency Operations Center (EOC). Expected actions: (1) Append earthquake status to ALL 5 building Name fields — RED for severe, YELLOW for moderate, GREEN for minor. (2) Create exactly 3 new corrective maintenance work orders — 2 Critical for the severe buildings mentioning 'Structural Assessment', 1 High for the moderate building mentioning 'MEP Systems Inspection'. (3) Do NOT create work orders for the minor buildings. (4) Create 1 new floor 'EOC — Temporary Operations' (code FLR-EOC-01) under the EOC building. (5) Create 3 rooms under that floor: RM-EOC-CMD (Command Room), RM-EOC-COM (Communications Hub), RM-EOC-SUP (Supply Staging). (6) WO-SEISMIC-TEST is a pre-existing routine calibration work order (visible in Corrective Maintenance as 'WO-SEISMIC-TEST: Annual seismic sensor calibration') — it must NOT be modified, deleted, or confused with earthquake response. The demo database has 5 buildings: WB01 (Office Building 01, Florence — SEVERE), WB02 (Office Building 02, Florence — MODERATE), CH01 (Chalman House, Red Bank — MINOR/EOC), WB14 (Duplex Apartment 14, Rome — SEVERE), TT01 (Tecnoteca headquarters, Tavagnacco — MINOR). Work orders are created via the Corrective Maintenance process in the sidebar.
